Gegg Yamachika
Assistant City Manager
City of La Puente
15900 E. Main Srreet
La Puente, CA9l744
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-09-178
Dear Mr. Yamachika:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding your duties as La puente
:t^t:1,1?, "i:1,.T1g". under the conflict-of-interest provisions of thi political Reform Act (the
"Act")' and the duties of La Puente lnterim City Manager Frank Tripepi. Because your question
is general in nature, we are treating your request as one for informal ur]ri.tura". please note, our
advice is limited to the provisions of the Aci, and we cannot advise you with respect to other
conflict of interest provisions, such as Government Code Section 1090 -- which is outside the
Act. Also, please note that the Commission does not act as a finder of fact when it renders
advice. (ln re Oglesby (1975) 1 FppC Ops. 71,77.)
QUESTION
May you recommend to the city council that the city contract with a firm to provide
engineering, building and safety services, despite the fact that the firm is the parent company of a
business that employs, and is a source of income to, the city manager?
CONCLUSION
- 
According to your facts, you would not be prohibited from making a recommendation to
the city council concerning a contract with the firm.
' The Political Refbrm Act is contained in Government code Sections g1000 through gl0l4. All statutory
ret'erences are to the Government code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair political practices
CommissionarecontainedinSections l8ll0throughlSggTofTitle2of trriCaliforniaCodeofRegulations. All
regulatory ref'erences are to Title 2, Division 6 of thl California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.


Our File No. I-09-l7g
page2
You are the Assistant city Manager fol the City of La puente. La puente Interim city
Manager is Frank Tripep-i' The city is currently consid'ering a contract with a private firm to
provide engineering, building and iafetyservices t9 the "ul TI" firm is the parenr company of
Mr' Tripepi's employer. Dui to his relationship wittr itre sibsidiary dr;;;, 
Mr. Tripepi has
excluded himself from participation and involve..rt in tt " 
Request for proposals, interviews,
analysis of proposals., and selection process. As Assistant city Manager, you wish to make a
recommendation to the council to contract with the firm.
FACTS
ANALYSIS
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions ensure that public officials will perform their
duties in an impartial manner, free from bias.caused uv,rr"ir financial interests. (section
81001(b)') specifically, section 87100 prohibitrany puuri. official fro,,,;*uking,,,
"participating in making," or otherwise using his or'her official position to ..influence,, 
a
governmental decision in which the official f,as a financial interest. In deiermining whether an
official has a conflict of interest under the Act, tt " .o--irsion utilize, u ,tunoura eighrstep
analysis. (Regulation 18700 (bxl) _ (8).)
Step One: Are you a..public officialr,?
The first step is met under your facts. The Act's .o:lJ.lgfinterest provisions apply to
"public officials'" (sections 87100, 87\}3;Regulation rszoo (b) (l).) As city manager and
assistant city manager, you are both 'public orrtiutr.;i (Sections gz04g, gzo4l).
Step Two: Witl you be "makingr'r,.participating in makingr or,.influencing,, a
governmental decision?
The Act's conflicrof-interest provisions apply only to disqualify a public official from
"making," "participating in making,"'or..influenciriy u g;u.-rn"ntal decision. If the
determination is made that the public official i:1";;;Eirg in any of these actions, then the
conflict-of-interest 3l9s are noi implicated.andgr;"!;is eras. Therefore, the threshold
:;fi:':l"llfll ;:Xilffi:#i:,3;;,*.o,bric 
ornciar is"?naking," .puni"ip-u,i,,'#il#,,lr,,, ,,
Making a Governmental Decision: llnder the analysis prescribed by step two, a public
official "makes a governmental decision" when ,h" ;ffi.;i actlng within ,t, ur,t ority of his or
her office or position, votes on a matter, appoints a person,obligates or commits his or her
agency to any course of action, or enters inlo any contractual agieement o..r}rurrof his or her
agency. (Section 87100; Regulation lg702.l(a).)
Participattng in Making a Governmental Decision: A public official .,participates in
making a governmental decision" when, acting within the authority of his or hir position and


Our File No. I-09-178
page 3
without significant substantive or intervening review, the official negotiates, advises or makes
recommendations to the decision-maker regarding the govemmental decision. (section g7100;
Regulation 19702.2.)
lnfluencing a Governmental Decision: Thereare two rules as to whether a public official
uses or attempts to use his or her official position to influence a governmental decision. The first
rule' which applies to your question, applies to ar govemmentar decisions within or before your
own agency' or an agency appointed by or subject io the budgetary 
"on,rJ oiyou, ug"n"y. '---
(Regulation 187o2.3(a).) In that 
"are, if "the dfficiar 
"oriu"ir', * ;#;r;;i"re, or otherwise
attempts to influence, any member, officer, employee or consultant of the agency,, then he or she
is attempting to influence a govemmental decision. rhi, includes, uut ir ro?limited to,
"appearances or contacts by the official on behalf of a businer. 
".riiiv, "ii;;;'", customer.,,
(rbid.)
You stated you intend to participate in, or use your official position to influence, the
decision before the city council by making u r".o*-"ndution that the city contract with the firm.
with respect to Mr. Tripepi, you stated that he "has excluded himself entirely from
participation and involvement in ihe Request for rroposats, interviews, analysis of proposals,
and selection process.'. Assuming he has not "influe'n."J.. trr. decisions u, ,., forth above, he
would not have a conflict of interest as defined in the Act despite having an economic interest in
the decision as discussed below.
Step Three: What are your economic interests?
section 87103 provides that a public official has a "financial interest,, in a govemmental
decision "if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material iinanciat effect,
distinguishable from its effect ol the public-generarrv, o" irre official, a member of his or her
immediate family," or on any of the official'i ..ono.ni. inierests. There are six possible
economic interests:
1' A public official has an economic interest in a business entity in which he or she has a
direct or indirect investment of $2,000 or more (section g7103(a); Regulation 1g703.1(a));
2' A public official has an economic interest in which he or she is a director, officer,
partner' trustee, employee, or holds any position of management (section g7103(d); Regulation
18703.1(b));
3' A public official has an economic interest in real property in which he or she has a
direct or indirect interest of $2,000 or more (section g71036); itegutation taio3.zl;
4' A public official has an economic interest in any source of income, including
promised income, which aggregates to $500 or more within l2 months prior to the decision
(Section 87103(c); Regulation iSZO:.:);


Our File No. I-09-17'8
page 4
5' A public official has an economic interest in any source of gifts to him or her if the
gifts aggregate to $420 or more within l2 monthr pri"r ait" decision (Section g7103(e);
Regulation 18703.4);
6' A public official has an economic interest in his or her personal expenses, income,
assets' or liabilities, as well as those of his or her immediaie family. rrris is rJrown as the
"personal financial effects" rule (section g7103, negulation 1g703.5).
You stated that Mr' Tripepi wasemploled by a subsidiary of the business that was being
considered for a contract with thJ city. unoer Reguiationlsz03.l(c) and 1g703. 3(a)(2), an
official has an economic interest in any busi".y .litity*irir, is a parent or subsidiary of, or is
otherwise related to, a business in which the official liur ; economic interest as either a business
entity or a source of income. Because the parent or rvrr. irip"pi', employer would be an
applicant before the city council, Mr. Tripepi would rrur" * economic interest in the decision
trj::j}|[ffi,:trf"perlv 
disqualiried himself no." fu'ti"ipation in the i".iriom affecting
You have not described any economic interest you may have in the decision in question.
Moreover, conflict-of-interest responsibilities are p".roruiio trr. official and are not imputed to
co-workers' Thus, Mr' Tripepi's Lconomic interesi does not create a conflict of interest for you.
If you do not have your own economic interest involved inthe decision, you wourd not have a
conflict of interest.
we have not gone on to analyze the remaining steps in the-eight-step analysis since you
do not appear to have an "economicinterest" in the dlcisi,on as definid in the Act and may,
therefore, participate in or use your official position to influence, the decision you describe.
I hope this information was helpful and provided some guidelines to follow. If you have
any other questions regarding this matter, please contact -e ut (916) 322_5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Counsel
W. Wallace
sistant General Counsel

QUESTION: May you recommend to the city council that the city contract with a firm to provide
engineering, building and safety services, despite the fact that the firm is the parent company of a
business that employs, and is a source of income to, the city manager?

CONCLUSION: - 
According to your facts, you would not be prohibited from making a recommendation to
the city council concerning a contract with the firm.
' The Political Refbrm Act is contained in Government code Sections g1000 through gl0l4. All statutory
ret'erences are to the Government code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair political practices

regulatory ref'erences are to Title 2, Division 6 of thl California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.
